exampleSentences: Thiệt tình, anh không biết chuyện gì đã xảy ra tối qua.
Em thiệt tình không hiểu tại sao mọi người lại giận như vậy.
Chị thiệt tình cảm thấy rất vui khi gặp lại mọi người hôm nay.
notes:
thiệt tình = honestly / seriously. It shows you’re being sincere, or a bit fed up while telling the truth.

It’s a sentence adverb, usually at the beginning to express real feelings or mild frustration. It adds emotion, not new information.  

Very close to thật tình (more common in the North). “Thiệt” sounds more Southern and a bit more emotional.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Honestly, I don't know what happened last night.
Honestly, I don't understand why everyone is so angry.
Honestly, I feel very happy to see everyone again today.
notes_md:
**thiệt tình** = honestly / seriously. It shows you’re being sincere, or a bit fed up while telling the truth.

It’s a sentence adverb, usually at the beginning to express real feelings or mild frustration. It adds emotion, not new information.  

Very close to **thật tình** (more common in the North). “Thiệt” sounds more Southern and a bit more emotional.
